description: Figure 7 presents the architecture of the genetic algorithm (GA) utilized for global hyper-parameter optimization of the Extra Trees model in data-driven yield strength prediction of medium-manganese low-density steels. The schematic depicts chromosome encoding and crossover operations through four rows: Parent 1 displays a complete nine-gene integer sequence [3, 1, 4, 2, 5, 8, 7, 6, 9] represented by alternating gray and light-blue blocks; Parent 2 shows a partially readable chromosome with dark-blue terminal segments and light-blue middle regions, though some allele values are unreadable. Offspring 1 exhibits a recombined sequence [3, 1, 4, 8, 2, 5, 7, 6, 9] where the central light-blue segment differs from Parent 1, illustrating gene exchange during crossover. Color-coded blocks distinguish chromosomal segments inherited from different parents. Offspring 2 is presented as an incomplete template below. This schematic substantiates the computational methodology wherein GA addresses high-dimensional, mixed-variable, non-convex optimization challenges for ensemble model hyper-parameters, enabling escape from local minima and supporting accurate yield strength predictions validated on Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si steel.
